basicDetails.schemeName: Pension Scheme for Elderly, Destitute Artists, Litterateurs and Writers of Uttarakhand State

schemeContent.detailedDescription_md: The scheme “Pension Scheme for Elderly, Destitute Artists, Litterateurs, and Writers of Uttarakhand” was introduced by the Department of Culture and Endowments, Government of Uttarakhand. The scheme aims to provide financial assistance to elderly and impoverished artists, litterateurs and writers who have dedicated their lives to the development of art, culture, and literature and have made significant and distinguished contributions.
schemeContent.benefits_md: The department provides a monthly pension of ₹3,000/- to the beneficiaries under this scheme.
schemeContent.briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide financial assistance to elderly and impoverished artists, litterateurs and writers who have dedicated their lives to the development of art, culture, and literature and have made significant and distinguished contributions.

children.text: The scheme “Pension Scheme for Elderly, Destitute Artists, Litterateurs, and Writers of Uttarakhand” was introduced by the Department of Culture and Endowments, Government of Uttarakhand. The scheme aims to provide financial assistance to elderly and impoverished artists, litterateurs and writers who have dedicated their lives to the development of art, culture, and literature and have made significant and distinguished contributions.
The department provides a monthly pension of ₹3,000/- to the beneficiaries under this scheme.
To avail the benefits under the pension scheme, applicants can register and apply through the “Apuni Sarkar Uttarakhand” portal available at

. Along with the application form, the eligible applicant must submit complete details of themselves and their family, along with necessary documents such as Aadhaar card, permanent residence certificate of Uttarakhand, PAN card, bank account details, educational qualification certificate, caste certificate, birth certificate or age certificate issued by a medical officer, a certificate related to artist/litterateur/writer issued by a government, semi-government, non-government or other recognized institution, a certificate of being an artist, and a recommendation from the district administration.
The process is considered complete only after these documents are uploaded on the portal by signing up at the "Apuni Sarkar Uttarakhand” Portal,

. After the submission, the applications are scrutinized by the Director General/Director and then presented before a committee constituted under the chairmanship of the Secretary/Principal Secretary. The committee verifies the submitted documents and calls the concerned artist for an interview. If found eligible during the interview and document verification, the pension is recommended. Subsequently, the Directorate of Culture disburses the approved pension amount to the artist every month.

In the event of the death of an artist or litterateur, the monthly pension is transferred to their spouse as a dependent. At present, the Directorate of Culture is providing monthly pension benefits to a total of 123 elderly and indigent artists.

applicationProcess.process_md: - To avail the benefits under the pension scheme, applicants can register and apply through the “Apuni Sarkar Uttarakhand” portal available at [https://eservices.uk.gov.in](https://eservices.uk.gov.in/). Along with the application form, the eligible applicant must submit complete details of themselves and their family, along with necessary documents such as Aadhaar card, permanent residence certificate of Uttarakhand, PAN card, bank account details, educational qualification certificate, caste certificate, birth certificate or age certificate issued by a medical officer, a certificate related to artist/litterateur/writer issued by a government, semi-government, non-government or other recognized institution, a certificate of being an artist, and a recommendation from the district administration. - The process is considered complete only after these documents are uploaded on the portal by signing up at the "Apuni Sarkar Uttarakhand” Portal, [https://eservices.uk.gov.in](https://eservices.uk.gov.in/). After the submission, the applications are scrutinized by the Director General/Director and then presented before a committee constituted under the chairmanship of the Secretary/Principal Secretary. The committee verifies the submitted documents and calls the concerned artist for an interview. If found eligible during the interview and document verification, the pension is recommended. Subsequently, the Directorate of Culture disburses the approved pension amount to the artist every month. - In the event of the death of an artist or litterateur, the monthly pension is transferred to their spouse as a dependent. At present, the Directorate of Culture is providing monthly pension benefits to a total of 123 elderly and indigent artists.

eligibilityCriteria.eligibilityDescription_md: 1. The applicant must be an elderly, destitute artist, litterateur, or writer from Uttarakhand. 1. The applicant must have dedicated their life to the development of art, culture, and literature. 1. The applicant must have made significant and distinguished contributions in their field. 1. The applicant must be at least 60 years of age. 1. The applicant must be a native or permanent resident of Uttarakhand.

children.text: The applicant must be an elderly, destitute artist, litterateur, or writer from Uttarakhand.
The applicant must have dedicated their life to the development of art, culture, and literature.
The applicant must have made significant and distinguished contributions in their field.
The applicant must be at least 60 years of age.
The applicant must be a native or permanent resident of Uttarakhand.
